The Operating Intelligence Layer for Automotive Retail

Your systems record what happened. ENGENIX helps change what happens next.

Follow the operating decisions behind every deal, from the desk and finance office through compliance, funding, controller review, and leadership action.

The operating gap

Every department sees a piece.
No system preserves the whole decision.

ENGENIX is designed to connect operating context across the life of the deal so the next action remains visible before time, judgment, and value disappear.

Systems of record

The dealership already has the data.
The decision is trapped between systems.

Each platform records its portion of the transaction. ENGENIX is the operating-intelligence layer designed to preserve what changed, why it matters, and who owns the next move.

CRMCustomer intentRecords the conversation
DMSTransaction recordRecords the deal
LENDERApproval contextRecords the callback
MENUProduct decisionRecords the presentation
DOCUMENTSContract evidenceRecords the packet
ACCOUNTINGFinancial postingRecords the outcome
ENGENIX OPERATING INTELLIGENCE What changed? Why does it matter? Who owns what happens next?
Illustrative operating model. Data access and integration pathways depend on each dealership's approved environment.
One deal · every operating layer

One Deal.
One Operating Truth.

The transaction does not restart when it changes departments. ENGENIX is designed to preserve the decisions, expectations, exceptions, and ownership surrounding it.

SYNTHETIC DEAL JOURNEYDeal #2847 · Jordan Taylor · 2026 SUV
09:14:02
DESKING INTELLIGENCE

The payment objective and selected structure do not align.

DECISION NEEDED

The current vehicle and term produce a payment above the customer's stated objective. A collateral rescue path is available before the deal stalls.

EXPECTED$725/mo
→
CURRENT$811/mo
→
NEXT MOVEReview alternate collateral
WHY IT MATTERSCustomer wait and deal viability

The intelligence architecture

Not six disconnected products.
Six views of the same operating truth.

Compliance enters first. The full ENGENIX architecture extends operating visibility across the life of the deal and the dealership.

Operational today Preparing for controlled deployment
01CONTROLLED DEPLOYMENT

Operating Command

Cross-department priorities, customer waits, financial exposure, approvals, and leadership decisions in one operating view.

OPERATOR QUESTIONWhat requires action now?
02CONTROLLED DEPLOYMENT

Desking Intelligence

Deal strategy, customer objectives, structure rescue, lender pathways, callbacks, approvals, and a protected F&I handoff.

OPERATOR QUESTIONWhat is the strongest path forward?
03CONTROLLED DEPLOYMENT

F&I Intelligence

Customer-declared ownership needs, product-fit context, menu readiness, presentation records, and funding assurance.

OPERATOR QUESTIONDoes the final ownership decision still fit?
04OPERATIONAL TODAY

Compliance Intelligence

Deal-jacket review, prioritized findings, corrective action, management visibility, and preserved decision history.

OPERATOR QUESTIONCan this deal defensibly move forward?
05CONTROLLED DEPLOYMENT

Controller Intelligence

Deal-to-ledger reconciliation, Controlled Variable, financial exceptions, accruals, liabilities, and close readiness.

OPERATOR QUESTIONDid the dealership retain what the deal promised?
06CONTROLLED DEPLOYMENT

Guided Experience

Role-based operating days, guided tasks, universal search, contextual explanations, and direct access to the full workspace.

OPERATOR QUESTIONWhat do I need to do next?
STRUCTURE→SELL→VERIFY→FUND→RECONCILE→CLOSE
The ENGENIX Guided Experience

Complexity belongs in the intelligence.
Not in the operator's day.

Most dealership employees should never have to learn where every function lives. ENGENIX is designed to present what matters, why it matters, and the next approved action for that person's role.

01Role-based prioritiesEach operator sees the work that belongs to them.
02Guided decisionsOne decision, one explanation, one clear next action.
03Full depth on demandExperienced operators can enter the complete workspace anytime.

Continuous Deal Reconciliation

From expected economics to financial truth.

Controller Intelligence is designed to explain how a deal moves from the desk's expectation through contract, funding, accounting, and realized economics without replacing the DMS, general ledger, or controller's judgment.

Preparing for controlled deployment. The public sequence shows outcomes and accountability, not proprietary reconciliation logic.
CONTROLLED VARIABLEILLUSTRATIVE DEAL #2847
DESK EXPECTED$4,880Approved structure
↓
CONTRACTED$4,620Final transaction
↓
FUNDED$4,410Lender proceeds
↓
BOOKED$4,350Accounting treatment
↓
REALIZED$4,310Retained economics
TimingFunding proceeds not yet posted$210
Known movementProduct-cost adjustment$260
Review requiredUnclassified variance$100
Operator control by design

Powerful intelligence.
Visible accountability.

ENGENIX supports dealership judgment. Qualified dealership personnel remain responsible for consequential decisions and approved actions.

01

Explainable context

Every surfaced issue should state what happened, why it matters, and the evidence available for review.

02

Human authority

ENGENIX prepares and guides. The dealership confirms, approves, documents, or overrides consequential actions.

03

Decision memory

Findings, approvals, exceptions, corrective actions, and completed responses remain attached to the operating record.

04

Role-based control

Access, visibility, and authority should reflect each operator's responsibility inside the dealership.

Founding operators

Built inside the pressure of automotive retail.
Not around generic software assumptions.

ENGENIX is being shaped by operators and systems leadership who understand the decisions, accountability, and workflow friction inside a dealership.

Founder & CEO

Nicholas Engen

“I spent approximately twenty years making dealership decisions under pressure. Then I built the operating intelligence I wish I had.”

Automotive retail operating experience across sales leadership, desking, F&I, lender relationships, and dealership execution.

Operator-firstAutomotive retail leadership
Chief Operating Officer

Miguel Figueroa

“Dealership software only works when it respects the pace, pressure, and accountability of the people using it.”

Operational leadership focused on execution discipline, dealership adoption, customer experience, and turning product vision into repeatable operating practice.

ExecutionOperational discipline and adoption
Chief Systems Engineer

Jacob Leyva

“The best systems make the right decision easier to see exactly when the pressure is highest.”

Systems engineering leadership focused on dependable architecture, information flow, security, and the infrastructure required for accountable operating intelligence.

SystemsArchitecture, reliability, and security
The operating principle

ENGENIX does not replace human judgment. It preserves judgment under pressure by keeping context, ownership, evidence, and the next action visible.

The Founding Dealership Network

The first layer is operating.
The complete system is entering the dealership.

ENGENIX is forming a selective network of automotive retailers prepared to deploy Compliance Intelligence and participate in controlled evaluation of the operating layers that follow.

Leadership-led briefing. Controlled product access. No generic software pitch.
